zero_slop_policy.statement: NO AI SLOP ALLOWED AT ALL - ZERO TOLERANCE TO SLOP AND HALLUCINATIONS
zero_slop_policy.strict_compliance: Mandatory. Every claim must be project-specific, evidence-backed, trace-linked, reviewer-challenged, and free of unsupported invention.
zero_slop_policy.missing_evidence_rule: If evidence is missing, mark it as an assumption, risk, open question, or failure.

created_by: df-meta-attractor invoked by claude-opus-4-7 orchestrator at user request 'multi day dark factory run to implement the wealth.com PRD rigorously'

source_signals.reference: Conversation message 2026-04-25: 'read thru the meta skills and skills - then read thru the wealth_com* prd and you will now do a multi day dark factory run to implement the prd rigorously in dark factory following everything in there without missing. absolute confirmance needed.'
source_signals.extract: User demands rigorous, complete dark-factory conformance for the wealth.com PRD implementation; multi-day run; subagents and deep web research permitted; SDLC discipline required.

Grok deep-research dump that captured wealth.com domain, surfaces, AI/Ester capability, tax/estate flows. Verbose; treated as primary source for PRD atom extraction (in flight via subagent a80dcae7dec19ae3a).

Live-capture catalog of wealth.com screens, products (Ester, Extractor, Family Office Suite, EstateFlow, Heritage Map, Ownership Balance Sheet, Estate Tax Projections, Report Builder, Scenario Builder), 19+ integrations, 15-leader org chart, $65M Series B led by Schwab (Apr 14 2026), 2025/2026 stat claims (>100k docs, >1k deterministic calcs/estate). Read in full into context.

Wealth.com YouTube channel inventory: S2 episodes, Practical Planner Podcast, third-party demos with Danny Lohrfink. Domain content: irrevocable trusts, tax-vs-estate, executor role, charitable giving, dynasty trusts.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2017, 15289:2019, 42001:2023, NIST SSDF 800-218, OWASP SAMM, OMG MDA/UML 2.5.1, CMMI V3.0 — accepted as governance spine.

source_signals.reference: 14 dark-factory skill bundles in C:\Users\abhir\version2\ (df-* + dark-factory-orchestrator) read into context
source_signals.extract: Skill system mandates Attractor Run Record + Engagement Governance + KG seed + TASKS.md bead ledger + TPM flow ledger + PERT plan + AI judge/jury policy + execution-kernel preflight + SDLC Stage Coverage Matrix + Product Tailoring Profile + Generated Meta-Skill Contract + Hawkeye Conformance Audit Record before material work.

attractor_state_rationale: [Updated 2026-04-26 post-checkpoint-1] Originally `forming`. Now stable: (1) engagement governance approved at checkpoint-#1 with all 9 HDs accepted via 'approve all defaults', (2) PRD atomization complete with 212 cited atoms (TB-WC-2026-0425-002), (3) domain brief complete with 165 citations + 6 critical findings (TB-WC-2026-0425-002B / agent a32d635dba92a1491), (4) skill survey complete (TB-WC-2026-0425-001), (5) tech stack confirmed via HD-003, (6) deployment target confirmed via HD-004 (local Docker Compose), (7) Hawkeye-Stage0 independent CONDITIONAL_PASS with patches applied. Stage-1 intake unblocked. Original ASM-003 INVALIDATED and superseded by FACT-OBBBA-001 (client accepted via HD-001 default).
goal: Build a rigorously-engineered, standards-conformant, evidenced clone of the wealth.com platform — Ester AI extraction, Document Creation, Scenario Builder, Tax Planning, Family Office Suite, integrations hub, dashboards, and reporting — through a governed dark-factory run that produces both a working software system AND a complete project book (BRD, SRS, NFR catalog, HLD, LLD, ADRs, DDD model, traceability matrix, runbooks, certificates, Hawkeye audit) traceable from human intent down to executed tests.
scope_in: All 14 wealth.com surfaces inventoried in wealth_com_PRD_artifacts.md §1: Homepage, Document Creation, Scenario Builder, Ester AI, Tax Planning, Platform Overview, Integrations, Pricing, For Advisors/Enterprises/Attorneys/Trust Companies, About, Features Overview.

Core entity model from PRD §9 cheat sheet: Household, People & Entities, Holdings & Assets, Financial Accounts, Insurance & Liabilities, Documents & Records, Tax Profile & Forms, Plans & Recommendations, Activity & Audit, Tasks.
Five core workflows from PRD §9: Estate Drafting, Estate Scan/Extract (Ester), Tax Baseline, Scenario Builder, Reports.
Trust strategies modeled: GRAT, SLAT, ILIT, CLAT, Dynasty, Revocable Living, QPRT, IDGT, CRT/CRUT (per Scenario Builder evidence in PRD §1.3).

Integration adapters (mocked or contract-only at v0; real for Plaid-substitute and one custody): Addepar, Schwab, eMoney, Orion, Salesforce, Wealthbox, Carta, Coinbase, Yodlee, Zillow, Zocks, Black Diamond, Microsoft Entra, Microsoft Dynamics, Redtail, Goldman Sachs Custody, Google, Advyzon (19 listed).

AI capabilities: document extraction (Ester Extractor), executive summarization, deterministic estate calculations (federal/state estate tax, GST, exemptions, marital deduction, step-up basis), tax baseline calc, scenario projection, voice-to-text intake.
Compliance posture: SOC 2 Type II posture (controls designed; audit out of scope of this run), GLBA, CCPA/CPRA, multi-tenant isolation, encryption at rest/transit, audit log, RBAC.
Reporting: Executive Summary, Standalone Visual Report, Estate Report PDF, Form 706/709 worksheet exports.
Project book: BRD, SRS, NFR catalog, HLD, LLD per bounded context, ADR set, DDD ubiquitous-language map, trace matrix, test plan, runbooks, release notes, quality certificates.
Hawkeye conformance audits at every stage exit and at run closure.
scope_out: Live partner integrations to Schwab/Addepar/eMoney/etc. — these require contractual agreements out of scope for this run; PRD-level contract specs and mocks are in-scope.
Actual SOC 2 audit engagement, third-party pen test, formal legal opinion — controls and posture are designed; certification is out of scope.
Live customer onboarding, marketing pages, sales process — single demo tenant for evidence; production traffic out of scope.
Mobile native apps — web-only at v0 (UI surface flag = web).
i18n/multi-currency beyond USD — out of scope at v0 (recorded as deferred risk).

Trademark/brand assets that are wealth.com property — clone uses generic naming (e.g., 'Estate Compass' or similar) and Ester-substitute internal name; no use of registered marks Ester®, Family Office Suite™, EstateFlow™, Heritage Map, Plan for Every Tomorrow™. Confirm with client.
Live regulatory filings or use of platform with real client PII — synthetic data only.
Specific YouTube video transcripts not yet captured (recorded as a deferred research bead).

product_specific: Wealth.com surface inventory and entity model.
Estate/tax calculation rules and IRS-form mappings.
Ester AI extraction prompts and deterministic-calc layer.
factory_reusable: DDD bounded-context discipline applied to a regulated fintech AI platform.
AI extraction + deterministic-calc grounding pattern (PRD-extract → JSON schema → calc engine → narrative).
Multi-tenant SaaS skeleton with audit log, RBAC, SOC 2-aligned controls.
Scenario-modeling pattern that compares strategies via deterministic projection.
meta_meta_governance: Validation that the dark-factory skill system handles a regulated, AI-heavy, multi-surface, multi-tenant SaaS without overfitting to the prior benchmark workload.
Stress test of the Hawkeye conformance audit against a real-sized run.
Capture lessons that update the skill system (df-feedback-learning) — e.g., add fintech-specific critic personas and tax-rule sourcing rubrics.

selected_mode_rationale: Greenfield code build (wealth.com clone) operating under meta-meta governance because: the PRD demands full SDLC artifact set; user explicitly demanded 'absolute conformance' with the dark-factory skills; the run will exercise and validate the skill system against a high-risk regulated workload.

Engagement governance, RASCI, gate policy, change control, validators.
Full lifecycle orchestration after attractor stable.
Recursive spec decomposition of the PRD into testable leaves.
Blend RUP+DDD+TDD+SRE+SSDF for a regulated fintech AI SaaS.
BRD, SRS, NFR, HLD, LLD, ADR, runbooks, project book.
Three-expert debate per material design; AI judge/jury per state transition.
Multi-day session context packs, predecessor recovery.
Client checkpoints, async review handling, taste gates.
Release readiness, runbooks, observability, outage drill.

node_contract.what: Build a wealth.com-equivalent, multi-tenant, regulated fintech AI SaaS clone with end-to-end traceability from human intent through production-readiness, while validating the dark-factory skill system at scale.
node_contract.why: User demands rigorous, conformance-first SDLC discipline; user has invested in building the dark-factory skill system; wealth.com is a high-risk, AI-heavy, regulated workload that will stress-test the system; success produces both a software clone and a battle-tested factory.
node_contract.how: Greenfield-with-meta-meta-governance mode: meta-attractor → governance-mayor (engagement) → intake-spec-lab → methodology-blender → artifact-factory (HLD/LLD/ADR) → construction (TDD per vertical slice) → quality-refinery (RALPH) → traceability-evidence → production-sre-handoff. Each stage gated by judge/jury verdict and Hawkeye audit.
node_contract.where: C:\Users\abhir\version2\runs\wealth-com\ for all artifacts; runs/wealth-com/app/ for code; trace ledger at runs/wealth-com/09_traceability/.
node_contract.when: Multi-day, paced by client checkpoints. Stages: now (recon+attractor), Day 1 (governance, intake), Day 2-3 (elaboration, methodology, artifacts), Day 3-N (construction vertical slices), Day N+1 (transition, retrospective, Hawkeye final).
node_contract.who: Accountable: client owner = user (eranti@gmail.com per session env). Delivery owner: dark-factory orchestrator (claude-opus-4-7). Reviewers: AI personas instantiated in df-swarm-coordination per artifact panel matrix. Hawkeye auditor: independent persona (cannot be the same role that authored the audited item).
node_contract.how_good: 98% governance compliance, 96%/expert minimum, 100% mandatory traceability, 0 unresolved P0/P1 security findings, all required SDLC stages either accepted/waived/not-applicable with rationale. No documents-only acceptance — code/UI/scenario evidence mandatory.

system_theorist_and_meta_architecture_cr.persona_contract: Senior systems theorist with 25+ years across complex socio-technical systems and meta-cognitive architectures. Lens: attractors, recursion, transferability, anti-overfit, feedback. Decision rights: reject overfit to wealth.com, reject infinite-recursion factory designs, demand transfer-test commitments. Non-negotiables: factory must work for future projects unchanged. Red flags: hard-coding wealth.com nouns in rubrics; treating Series B funding context as durable. Artifact ownership: this record's `layer_map` and `anti_overfit_checks`. Adversarial duty: prove the run is overfitting whenever a wealth.com-only assumption is encoded.
system_theorist_and_meta_architecture_cr.framing: Attractor identified: the user wants both a product clone AND a validated factory. Risk: collapse into product-only mode (overfitting) or process-only mode (paper factory). Recommendation: split layer_map explicitly (done), require transfer tests (a fintech-adjacent test workload — e.g., 'Holistiplan-style tax baseline for solo CFP') as a deferred bead. Mark wealth.com noun-vocabulary as product-specific; mark the AI-extract+deterministic-calc pattern as factory-reusable. Stop conditions: if any wealth.com-specific artifact (e.g., 'GRAT') becomes a default rubric in df-artifact-factory's library, escalate.
rejected_alternatives: Run as pure greenfield without meta-meta governance — rejected: skips the user's explicit demand for 'absolute conformance'.
Run as artifact-only paper exercise — rejected: skill system explicitly forbids documents-only completion for code-producing/UI/data/production surface flags.

requirements_governance_architect.persona_contract: Lead requirements engineer / governance architect with deep experience in regulated fintech SDLC. Lens: customer intent, standards tailoring, interrogation, approval. Decision rights: reject runs without engagement governance, reject undocumented assumptions, demand client checkpoints for material spend. Non-negotiables: RASCI signed; threshold reductions blocked without owner waiver. Red flags: PRD treated as final spec without interrogation; wealth.com Series B claims encoded as requirements without source verification. Artifact ownership: scope_in/scope_out, gates list, human_decisions. Adversarial duty: prove that any 'accepted' requirement lacks source.
requirements_governance_architect.framing: Engagement governance not yet established — material token spend is gated. Need: client owner confirmation, scope baseline (drafted here), token SWAG band, change-control policy, reapproval triggers. Recommend: client checkpoint #1 before stage-intake (ask user to confirm scope_in/out, tech stack preference, deployment target, brand-naming substitution). Standards tailoring: include ISO 42001 (AI management) given Ester is a primary surface. Interrogation must convert PRD claims (e.g., '1,000 deterministic calculations per estate distribution') into testable leaf requirements. Stop conditions: ambiguous ownership for production data; legal scope on 'unauthorized practice of law' boundary for document-creation surface — escalate to user.
rejected_alternatives: Skip engagement governance because user said 'do it' — rejected: skill rules require approved engagement record before material spend.
Encode PRD verbatim as SRS — rejected: PRD is a research dump, not a validated spec; recursive decomposition required.

verification_safety_critic.persona_contract: Senior assurance engineer with regulated software (medical-device + fintech) background. Lens: proof, gates, residual risk, audit. Decision rights: reject template-as-proof, demand evidence for every claim, reject benchmark-only wins without holdout. Non-negotiables: P0/P1 security findings = 0 unresolved; 100% trace coverage. Red flags: 'we'll add tests later'; 'this is just a demo'. Artifact ownership: gates, evidence_commitments, anti_overfit_checks. Adversarial duty: prove every accepted artifact lacks instantiated evidence.
verification_safety_critic.framing: This workload has high safety/money/privacy/security/compliance/reliability risk because: (a) financial planning errors carry fiduciary liability, (b) tax calculations carry IRS/state penalty exposure, (c) document creation crosses UPL line, (d) HCD docs contain PHI subject to HIPAA, (e) AI extraction hallucination affects irrevocable client decisions. Required: deterministic-calc audit layer separate from LLM, golden-dataset tests for every tax/estate rule, holdout scenarios per trust strategy, transfer test on edge case (e.g., 'married couple with property in two estate-tax states + minor children + non-US-citizen spouse' → tests portability + QDOT + minor-trust + state nexus). Stop conditions: proceeding to production-surface evidence without outage drill record; certifying any gate without RALPH 5-loop completion.
rejected_alternatives: Use LLM for tax calculations directly — rejected: must be deterministic with LLM only for narrative.
Lower thresholds for v0 demo — rejected: skill rules require owner-scoped waiver and that defeats the validation purpose.

engagement_partner_token_budget.persona_contract: Outsourcing engagement partner accountable for client trust and budget integrity. Lens: rough token SWAG, checkpoint cadence, change control, reapproval. Decision rights: reject material work without an approved budget; require checkpoint-#1 before any spend > low-band SWAG. Non-negotiables: 15% delta or scope change requires reapproval. Red flags: 'just continue' framing without explicit budget approval; over-runs justified by user enthusiasm. Artifact ownership: token_budget block in engagement-governance-record. Adversarial duty: prove the iteration's spend exceeds approved band.
engagement_partner_token_budget.framing: User said 'multi day run' and 'invoke subagents whatever' — generous, but not unbounded. Recommend rough SWAG: low ~150K tokens, mid ~400K, high ~1.2M for the full run end-to-end (across multiple sessions, with subagents being the major consumers). Checkpoint-#1: NOW (after attractor pack arrives), confirm budget band and brand-substitution. Checkpoint-#2: end of intake/elaboration before construction begins. Checkpoint-#3: per vertical slice or weekly. Reapproval triggers: any of (scope change, +15% token forecast, threshold reduction, production-surface action, brand or naming change, integration with real partner). Stop conditions: spend approaches mid-band without checkpoint-#2; any irreversible action without explicit approval.
rejected_alternatives: Open-ended budget — rejected: violates engagement-governance policy.
Skip checkpoint-#1 because user already authorized — rejected: scope, brand, and tech-stack ambiguities make explicit confirmation necessary.

round_2_cross_critique.theorist_to_governance: You correctly demanded engagement governance, but the PRD's '$65M Series B' and '664% growth' framing risks becoming a factory-level rule about hyper-growth fintech. Strip those signals to product-context only.
round_2_cross_critique.governance_to_theorist: Agreed; layer_map already isolates them. But you must also add a transfer-test bead — abstract attractors are insufficient if they aren't proven against a second workload.
round_2_cross_critique.verification_to_theorist_and_governance: Both of you are too process-focused; without a deterministic-calc layer mandated as an architectural constraint at HLD time, the construction stage will produce LLM-driven calculations that hallucinate tax outcomes. Encode that as a non-negotiable architectural ADR slot now.
round_2_cross_critique.engagement_to_all: All three of you are designing the perfect run. Without a token budget approved by the client owner, this becomes an unbounded research project. Demand checkpoint-#1 before any further authored content beyond this attractor pack.
round_2_cross_critique.verification_to_engagement: Agreed in principle, but the attractor pack itself is necessary to anchor the checkpoint conversation. Authoring it before checkpoint is correct; authoring construction code is not.
round_2_cross_critique.theorist_to_verification: Add: holdout dataset for AI extraction must be sourced from public estate-document corpora to avoid data-leak from PRD examples. This is a transfer-test concern.

round_3_synthesis.decision: PROCEED through Stage 0 (recon, attractor pack, engagement governance draft, intake setup) under attractor_state=forming. STOP at the boundary of Stage 1 (intake material work) for client checkpoint-#1 to confirm: (a) scope_in/out as listed, (b) brand-substitution naming, (c) tech-stack preference (default proposed: TypeScript/Node + Postgres + React + Python AI service), (d) deployment target (default proposed: containerized for local demo, no cloud production), (e) token budget band approval, (f) integrations to mock vs. real, (g) UPL/legal-boundary handling for document-creation surface.
non_negotiable_constraints_locked_in: Deterministic calc layer architecturally separated from LLM (ADR at HLD time).
Golden-dataset tests for every tax/estate calc.
Holdout dataset for Ester extractor must come from public corpora, not PRD examples.
Synthetic data only for v0; no real PII or PHI.
Brand substitution required (no use of registered wealth.com marks).
Hawkeye conformance audit at every stage exit.
Client checkpoint #1 before stage-intake; #2 before construction; #N per vertical slice.
Transfer test bead deferred but mandatory before run closure (validates factory generalization).
residual_risks_carried_forward: Token budget could be unstable until checkpoint-#1 — mitigated by stopping authoring at end of Stage 0.
Brand name choice could change once user picks — mitigated by isolating naming in a single config.
Subagent reports may surface scope additions — handled via change-control beads.
Multi-day run risks context loss — mitigated by mandatory df-context-memory packs at every resume.
rejected_alternatives_archive: Run construction in same session — rejected: violates engagement gate.
Skip transfer test — rejected: violates anti-overfit gate.
Skip Ester deterministic-calc separation — rejected: violates safety/verification gate.

gates.criterion: Goal one sentence, scope explicit in/out, examples labeled, open questions separated, next safe action concrete.
Distinguishes product/meta-skill/meta-meta layers; no infinite planning; defines stop-and-execute boundary; records re-entry triggers.
Skills selected by need, each with reason, no unnecessary loading, human decision points identified.
Requirements have traceable sources, gates have evidence expectations, quality thresholds named, residual risks preserved, fresh reviewer can replay.
gates.evidence: §source_signals (every signal has reference); §gates with evidence pointer; §expert_debate.round_3_synthesis.residual_risks_carried_forward.

gates.criterion: Benchmark details don't become universal rules; transfer tests specified; domain-specific assumptions labeled.
gates.evidence: §layer_map (product vs factory_reusable); §anti_overfit_checks; transfer-test bead in TASKS.md (TB-WC-2026-0420).

gates.criterion: Attractor Run Record OR explicit waiver before governed work proceeds.
Approved engagement governance record before material token spend.
KG seed, TASKS.md, TPM flow ledger, PERT plan instantiated.
gates.evidence: KG-WEALTHCOM-001, TASKS.md, TPM-FLOW-WEALTHCOM-001, PERT-WEALTHCOM-001 (forthcoming in this commit).

gates.criterion: Project-type flags declared; SDLC Stage Coverage Matrix complete.
Product Tailoring Profile + Generated Meta-Skill Contract + Dark Factory Instantiation Record produced.
Initial Hawkeye Conformance Audit Record covering Stage-0 outputs.
gates.evidence: HAWKEYE-WEALTHCOM-001-stage0 (forthcoming after this commit; auditor persona must be independent of authoring persona).

gates.criterion: RALPH 5-loop minimum on every governed artifact before pass.
Code/product/app/UI/scenario/production work cannot pass as documents only.
gates.evidence: SDLC matrix flags + implementation-execution-record.json + scenario-test-matrix.json + wysiwyg-browser-test-record.json (per surface flag).

human_decisions.question: Confirm scope_in/scope_out as drafted in §scope_in/§scope_out (proceed / amend / reject).
Brand substitution: pick name (default proposal: 'EstateCompass' with internal AI 'Sage'); confirm no use of wealth.com registered marks.

Tech stack confirmation. Default proposal: Node 20 + TypeScript + Fastify; React 18 + Vite + Tailwind; Postgres 16; Python 3.12 FastAPI for AI service; Anthropic Claude for Ester-substitute extraction; OpenTelemetry; Docker Compose for local; Playwright for UI evidence; Vitest+Pytest for unit/integration. Approve or amend.
Deployment target. Default proposal: Docker Compose local-only at v0; production deployment out of scope. Approve.
Token-budget SWAG. Proposal: low 150K, mid 400K, high 1.2M tokens across full multi-day run including subagents. Approve band; reapproval at +15% delta or scope change.
Integration approach: mock 17 of 19, contract+mock for Plaid-substitute and one custody (Schwab-shape). Approve or amend list.

UPL (unauthorized practice of law) handling for Document Creation surface. Default: copy is generated as 'educational draft for attorney review'; no execution-ready legal documents emitted by AI; explicit attorney-of-record assignment required. Approve or amend.
Synthetic data only — no real PII/PHI in test or demo. Approve.

Hawkeye independence: auditor persona must be different from authoring persona. Default proposal: Hawkeye runs as a separate subagent with no read access to authoring transcripts beyond the artifact itself. Approve.

assumptions.statement: User wants this run to proceed multi-session with df-context-memory for resume safety.
Wealth.com PRD is the canonical source; Grok dump may contain hallucinations to be filtered during PRD-atom extraction.
assumptions.source: PRD file size (552KB) and 'deep research output' provenance
assumptions.revalidation_trigger: PRD-atom subagent flags >5% conflict ratio between PRD and external sources

assumptions.statement: [INVALIDATED 2026-04-26 by domain brief] 2025 federal estate tax exemption is ~$13.99M and 2026 reverts to ~$7M unless extended by Congress.

OBBBA (One Big Beautiful Bill Act) signed July 4, 2025 PERMANENTLY raised federal estate/gift/GST exemption to $15M (2026) with annual inflation adjustment. TCJA sunset that was scheduled for 12/31/2025 did not happen — the law was modified before it triggered. Top federal estate-tax rate remains 40%.
assumptions.source: domain-brief.md §1 with multiple primary citations to IRS and law firm publications
assumptions.revalidation_trigger: future legislative change or IRS guidance amendment
assumptions.client_decision_HD-001: Build clone to actual current OBBBA law, NOT PRD's repealed-TCJA framing.

assumptions.statement: NYDFS Cybersecurity Regulation 23 NYCRR Part 500 final amendments effective Nov 1, 2025; first compliance certification due April 15, 2026. Universal MFA mandatory; Asset Inventory Program required.

CCPA/CPRA July 2025 final regulations add Automated Decision-Making Technology (ADMT) rules effective Jan 1, 2027 — affects AI-driven recommendations including Ester/Sage tax/estate suggestions. Adds consumer-rights handling: transparency, opt-out, human review.
Montana and Connecticut eliminated GLBA entity-level exemption in 2025; fintechs holding consumer financial data in those states must comply with state privacy laws on top of GLBA.

Industry-canonical pattern for AI on regulated documents: LLM extracts structured facts → deterministic engine executes computations with audit trail → LLM is used only for narration. The PRD's '1,000 deterministic calculations per estate' claim aligns with this pattern.
Wealth.com's Ester is implemented as LLM + retrieval + deterministic calc layer; the '1,000 deterministic calculations per estate' claim implies a code-driven engine, not pure LLM.
assumptions.revalidation_trigger: evidence emerges that calculations are LLM-driven

assumptions.statement: User accepts AI personas as elite-role contracts (per skill system) rather than insisting on real human reviewers.

risks.title: AI extraction hallucination on legal documents leading to fiduciary harm.
risks.mitigation: Deterministic-calc layer separation (architectural ADR); citation grounding with span-level provenance; confidence-score thresholds with human-review fallback; holdout test set.

risks.title: Tax calculation error producing IRS/state penalty exposure.
risks.mitigation: Golden-dataset tests for every rule; deterministic engine; reviewed by Tax-Domain Juror persona at HLD and code time; explicit disclaimer surfaces.

risks.title: UPL — generated documents being mistaken for legal advice.
risks.mitigation: Per HD-007: 'educational draft for attorney review' framing; attorney-of-record assignment required before execution-ready output; legal-disclaimer overlay.

risks.title: Multi-tenant data leak between advisor firms.
risks.mitigation: Tenant isolation at row-level + connection-level (Postgres RLS); per-tenant KMS keys; audit log; OWASP testing; ADR-locked tenant boundary.

risks.title: Overfit factory to wealth.com workload.
risks.mitigation: layer_map enforced; transfer-test bead mandatory; df-feedback-learning record at run end.

risks.title: Unbounded token spend.
risks.mitigation: Engagement governance + checkpoint cadence; subagent budgets capped per dispatch.

risks.title: Brand IP infringement.
risks.mitigation: Brand substitution per HD-002; no use of registered marks.

risks.title: Multi-day context loss.
risks.mitigation: df-context-memory pack at every resume; TASKS.md authoritative for next bead.

risks.title: PHI in Advance Health Care Directives — HIPAA implications.
risks.mitigation: Synthetic data only at v0; encryption at rest with PHI-segregated key; access log; BAA out of scope but architectural posture supports it.

risks.title: PRD claims unverified (e.g., 664% growth, $65M Series B specifics) become product requirements.
risks.mitigation: PRD-atom subagent classifies each claim; only `requirement` and `capability` atoms become spec leaves; growth/funding claims become `context` only.

evidence_commitments: Every requirement bead linked to ≥1 PRD atom OR domain-brief citation OR explicit assumption.
Every artifact gets RALPH 5-loop record + artifact-specific critic panel + ≥2 adversarial critics.
Every code change linked to ≥1 requirement and includes unit + integration + scenario test evidence per surface flag.
Every UI change linked to Playwright wysiwyg-browser-test-record + screenshot + console/network logs.
Every tax/estate calc linked to golden-dataset test record + IRS-form citation.
Every state transition gated by AI judge/jury record (TPM Judge + Evidence Clerk + 3 specialist jurors + Adversarial Prosecutor + Foreperson).
Every stage exit gated by Hawkeye Conformance Audit Record (independent persona).
Every client-facing handoff packaged via df-human-agent-handoff record.
Every iteration's token spend reported against approved SWAG band; 15% delta triggers reapproval.
anti_overfit_checks: Stop condition: any wealth.com noun (Ester, GRAT, Heritage Map) being added to a *factory* template or rubric. Allowed only in product-specific runs/wealth-com/* artifacts.
Stop condition: any reference to '$65M Series B' or '664% growth' surviving into requirements (must be context-only).

Transfer test: TB-WC-2026-0420 — apply factory to a different fintech surface (e.g., 'Holistiplan-style tax-baseline mini-app for a solo CFP') after wealth.com run closes. Validates factory generalization. Mandatory before run closure.
Hawkeye independence: auditor persona must not have authored the audited content. Enforced at subagent dispatch time (separate agent IDs).
PRD-atom traceability: each atom has source_excerpt; if a requirement cannot cite a source_excerpt or a domain-brief citation or an explicit ASM-NNN, it is rejected by Evidence Clerk.
Holdout dataset for Ester extractor must come from public corpora (e.g., open law-school sample wills), not PRD examples. Prevents data leakage.
next_safe_action: Author the remaining Stage-0 records in this commit: product-tailoring-profile.json, generated-meta-skill-contract.json, sdlc-stage-coverage-matrix.json, knowledge-graph-record.json, TASKS.md, tpm-flow-ledger.json, factory-pert-plan.json, ai-judge-jury-policy.json, execution-kernel-state.json, next-action-report.json, dark-factory-instantiation-record.json, hawkeye-conformance-audit-record-stage0.json, engagement-governance-record.json (DRAFT pending checkpoint-#1). After authoring, prepare client checkpoint-#1 prompt summarizing HD-001..HD-009 for user approval. STOP at checkpoint-#1 boundary; do NOT begin material intake/construction work until approval.
reentry_triggers: Subagent reports arrive (skill-survey, prd-atoms, domain-brief) → integrate into TASKS.md beads and re-run GATE-D-EVIDENCE.
Client approves checkpoint-#1 → transition attractor_state from forming to stable; release stage-intake bead.
User amends scope_in/scope_out → re-run GATE-A-COHERENCE and GATE-D-EVIDENCE; update layer_map.
Token forecast drift +15% → re-run engagement-governance approval.
Any P0/P1 finding from Hawkeye → re-enter df-feedback-learning and update affected artifacts.
verdict_rationale: Pass on Gates A/B/C/D/E (self-scored, pending Hawkeye independent audit). Conditional because: (a) engagement governance not yet client-approved (GATE-ENGAGEMENT blocked), (b) initial Hawkeye audit not yet run on this stage-0 pack, (c) subagent reports (skill-survey, prd-atoms, domain-brief) not yet integrated. The pass is conditional pending checkpoint-#1 and Hawkeye-stage0.
